observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Create an Inventory Committee whose members are temporarily relieved of all their usual duties to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP). This plan should include the specific assignments and duties of the committee members, the cut-off date, and a schedule that outlines the dates and locations of the inventory activities from start to finish, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
Submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days prior to the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities.
Instruct the Inventory Committee, GSO-designate, and Municipal Accountant to adhere to the procedural guidelines outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to ensure more accurate account balances for presentation in the financial statements; and
Prepare and process the necessary adjusting journal entries to derecognize non-existing or undocumented assets, in accordance with proper accounting and audit procedures outlined in Sections 7 and 8 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to take steps to transfer the buildings by formally requesting the SB to pass a resolution that authorizes the transfer of these assets to the designated recipient local government units and national agencies.
Furthermore, upon securing the SB resolution and necessary donation  documents, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the appropriate adjusting entry to formally recognize the donation. To prevent an artificial inflation of expenses in the current year – which could distort financial performance and potentially result in an operating loss – the adjustment should not be recorded as a current-year expense. Instead, since these assets pertain to prior periods, they should have been classified as donations in previous years. We suggest closing the accounts under Prior Period Adjustments. The recommended adjusting entry is as follows:
Prior Period Adjustment				xxx
Accumulated Depreciation				xxx
Buildings 						xxx
School Buildings					xxx
Lastly, to ensure comparability and consistency in financial reporting, the Municipal Accountant should prepare restated financial statements. These restated statements should accurately reflect the impact of the depreciation expenses recognized in previous years. This will enhance the reliability and transparency of financial information, enabling stakeholders to make well-informed comparisons across reporting periods and support financial decision-making.

To address the above audit observation, we strongly recommend increasing the number of personnel assigned to undertake inventory taking and maintain inventory records. The involved personnel should be well-informed of their duties and responsibilities as outlined in Section 122 of the MNGAS.
We have further recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct both the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain updated inventory records based on the receipt, acceptance, and issuance of inventory items as prescribed by the Manual;
Strictly follow the procedures set forth in Section 122 of the Manual; and
Conduct semestral (or oftener depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items to be reported in the RPCI which should be submitted to the Audit Team’s office.

Establish sufficient policies and procedures in conducting the physical count, in coordination with the Supply Unit, Inventory Committee, Accounting Unit and other key personnel,
Instruct the Supply Unit to create and maintain separate records of PPE items owned by the LGU and update the same every time there is a transaction or movement of PPE accounts,
Upon designation of an Inventory Committee, it will conduct physical count of PPE at least annually together with a COA representative, who will witness the inventory-taking and to prepare the RPCPPE which shall be submitted to the Auditor's Office not later than January 31 of each year, and
Direct the Accounting and Supply Units to reconcile the PPE account balances based on their respective reports/records at the end of each year to ascertain that all properties owned by the LGU are duly accounted for.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to identify and notify in writing the contractors or suppliers entitled to the overdue guaranty/security deposits. If the concerned contractors or suppliers claim, the Municipality should refund the guaranty/security deposits, otherwise, the retained amounts shall be forfeited in favor of the Municipality through reversion of the balance of the payable account.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Accountant:
Revert to the General Fund the amount of savings from DRRMF-CO in compliance with Section 5.1.11 of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 and be made available for use in the disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP;
Recognize the transfer of unspent current year DRRMF to trust fund using the prescribed accounting entries specified in Annex C of the same Circular in the succeeding reporting periods; and
Strictly comply with the guidelines of the aforementioned Circular relative to the accounting and reporting of LDRRM Fund.

We recommend the Management to require the AOs to completely and accurately account their total collections at the end of every business day and remit the full amount to the liquidating officer. We also enjoin the concerned liquidating officer to verify the accuracy and completeness of their collections and remittances to avoid discrepancies in the RCDs.
We recommended Management to require the concerned collecting officers to remit their collections daily and intact to the treasurer/liquidating officer, who shall then deposit the full amount intact and promptly to the authorized government depository bank (AGDB) to prevent possible loss, theft and/or misappropriation of collections.
Moreover, we recommended the Accountable Officers to reflect the actual dates of collections in the RCDs, Abstract of Collections and Deposits and cashbooks to ensure that all collections are completely monitored and deposited intact.

We recommended that Management, moving forward, shall observe the proper procedures in designation of the Petty Cash Custodian in compliance with the rules and regulations specified in the COA Circular No. 97-002 relative to the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the Management to:
Require the PCC to properly accomplish the PCV, including the Part I and II of the form upon request and liquidation of the petty cash;
Observe the proper and complete accomplishment of the required information in the Drivers' trip tickets; and
Submit the necessary documents required in Section 1.2.2, Annex A of COA Circular No. 2023-004 to support the expenses charged to PCF.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant as well as the concerned officials to pay only the actual registration fee and the maximum allowable DTE for conventions, trainings or seminars pursuant to NBC No. 563 and EO No. 77. Excess from allowable limit shall be at the expense of the participants.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MPDC and other concerned officials to have constant monitoring and evaluation on the implementation of development projects funded under the 20% Development Fund, taking immediate action should there remain unattained objectives, as in the case of the abovementioned projects. This would ensure that the fund is optimally utilized to achieve desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive thru the Municipal Treasurer to:
Cause the production and sending of formal demand letter/notice to delinquent taxpayers to apprise them of their tax liabilities;
Re-assess collection systems in place if still effective and set added measures to ensure collection efficiency; and
Enforce remedies provided for under Section 254 and 256 of the LGC which includes posting and publishing of notice of the delinquency and civil and judicial actions in collecting RPT and SET, if warranted by circumstances.
As for RPT and SET Receivables which will be assessed during the current period, we recommended the Sanggunian to consider granting discounts not exceeding 20% percent of the annual tax due to encourage advanced prompt payment from taxpayers pursuant to Section 251 of the LGC.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the GFPS and the GAD focal person to constantly monitor the implementation of GAD programs and activities even if they are endorsed or streamlined to a specific unit or office only,
Advise the Budget Office to correctly match incurred expenses to the corresponding program or activity covered, and
Advise the GFPS to properly accomplish the GAR pursuant to Annex E of the Joint Circular to ensure the report's reliability.
We further encourage the concerned officials to fully utilize the allocated budget for GAD to effectively respond to identified gender issues, concerns or needs in the Municipality.

We have recommended that, in the preparation of budget for the succeeding years, the Municipal Budget Officer together with the Municipal Accountant and the Local School Board to review carefully the proposed programs/projects/activities under SEF whether these are in accordance with budgetary needs for the operation and maintenance of public schools.
Further, there must be strict adherence to the allowable expenditures chargeable to SEF enumerated under Item 5.2.1 of JC No. 1 s.2017 (DepEd-DBM-DILG) and refrain from charging unrelated projects to ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of resources.

Management’s Action/s:
The Multi-Purpose Covered Court(MPCC)/Evacuation Center(EC) was constructed within the premises of Taratara Elementary School. Although mentioned as an evacuation center, its main function is a Multi-Purpose Court or Hall where the school can conduct various programs and activities in support to various sports activities of the school children. The importance of MPCC cannot be overemphasized, as a matter of fact it is very important facility or building that a school should have for the convenience of the teachers and school children during school events especially during rainy season.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The specific purpose of the project as suggested by the project title, “Multi-Purpose Covered Court/Evacuation Center” (MPCC/EC) does not conform with the guidelines on the allowable expenditures under SEF. It is further recommended that subsequent MPCC/EC projects should not be charged against SEF but rather use LDRRM Fund or 20% Development Fund if school facility is not the main purpose for which the project will be constructed.

We have recommended that the concerned officials of the Municipality strictly comply with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and RA 9710 or the Magna carta of Women to ensure that the programs, projects, and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We have recommended that the municipality through its GAD Focal Point maximize the utilization of the GAD Funds through substantial implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which the funds were provided.

We have recommended for the implementation of the ATM Payroll System by the Municipality to be able to avail the benefits of safety, timeliness and cost efficiency scheme in disbursing payroll to its employees. Further, if the Municipality is capable to do, install an ATM booth within the premise of the Municipal grounds to ease the access of cash withdrawal and encourage the townspeople, especially the government workers to avail the service. This instance may also lead to more economic activities within the Municipality premises since cash will be always available for withdrawal instead of going elsewhere to withdraw and shop.

In cases of monetization of more than 50% of accumulated leave credits, officials and employees must submit additional documentary requirements to support their request for monetization provided in Section 5.14 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure compliance with the requirements set forth under Section 52 of General Appropriation Act (GAA) of 2019 and adopt control measures to ensure that employee’s monthly net take home pay shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00) after deducting mandatory deductions and other obligations.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that inventory committee should conduct physical count of all the property, plant and equipment of the municipality and prepare a report on the result of the physical count. Subsequently, reconcile the report with the reported valuation of the properties presented in the financial statements and submit the same to the office of the auditor as prescribed under Section 124 of the NGAS, Volume I of the LGUs.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended to Management the following:
Direct all concerned officials and employees to settle immediately their unliquidated cash advances.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and other department heads concerned to observe consistently strict compliance with rules/regulations on the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances per COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 82 of PD 1445

We have recommended therefore that Management institute the following control measures:
Regular reconciliation of records pertaining to the under and over remittance of premium contributions;
Maintenance of employees' individual ledger cards to monitor the deductions of each employee; and
PhilHealth premium contributions deducted from employees must be remitted within the prescribed period.

We have recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit all PAG-IBIG contributions especially those pertaining to prior years to avoid delay in the processing of members'/employees' claims and prevent possible forfeiture of the benefits due them.
Likewise, regular review/analysis of the details of the subsidiary ledger balances of Due to Pag-IBIG account to determine the reason for the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustment/s thereof.

We have recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant allow only 30 days as the maximum number of days to be monetized pursuant to Section 22 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave.  In cases of monetization of more than 50% of accumulated leave credits, officials and employees must submit additional documentary requirements to support their request for monetization provided in Section 5.14 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure compliance with the requirements set forth under Section 52 of General Appropriation Act (GAA) of 2019 and adopt control measures to ensure that employee’s monthly net take home pay shall not be lower than Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000.00) after deducting mandatory deductions and other obligations.

In view of the above-mentioned observations, we have recommended the following:
That Management direct the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer to submit a copy of Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF using the format provided in Annex B of COA Circular No.2012-002 to facilitate  the review and monitoring of the sources and utilization of the fund.
That the concerned officials to ensure the regular and timely submission of the said report in compliance with the above-mentioned COA Circular.

In view of the above-cited observation, we have recommended the following:
That the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry to effect the 15% and 5% share of the OBO and DPWH, respectively from the collection of building permit;
That the Municipal Treasurer religiously monitor the amount to be automatically retained to be used for OBO’s programs and projects and the amount to be remitted to BTr representing the 5% share of DPWH; and
c.	Municipal Engineering Office as the OBO to prepare Program of Work detailing the proposed utilization of the 15% share from the collection of building permits and properly observe the guidelines in the preparation of the program of works.
We have recommended that Management give priority to revisit the Municipality’s 2007 Local Revenue Code and to actively work for its revisions, as necessary.

We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should endeavor to implement the programs/projects as listed in the Annual Investment Program to the fullest possible to give the townspeople the maximum benefits they could derived and to strictly adhere to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No. 2011-1.

We have recommended that utilization of GAD Fund be maximized and that proper planning be made so that all GAD related projects, programs and activities during the year be implemented immediately for the full attainment of its benefits beneficial to their constituents.

We have recommended for Management to institute the following control measures:
Instruct the concerned offices to reconcile the records pertaining to unremitted taxes withheld and to enforce immediately the remittance of overdue accounts, if any.
Remit intact all withheld taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in compliance to Revenue Regulations 2-98 and provisions of RA 8424 to avoid paying penalties, surcharges and interest arising from violations. Failure to do so may result to punishment as specified under Section 272 of the said Act.

1.7 We recommended and the following LGU personnel agreed that:
1.7.1 The Chairperson of the MPIADC to initiate the planning and
conduct comprehensive periodic physical inventory of ALL the supplies
and property of the LGU as mandated in Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual. Prepare and submit also the RPCI as a result of the inventory
taking of the supplies. The RPCPPE and RPCI shall be reconciled with
the accounting records. These Reports shall also be submitted to COA
pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU;
1.7.2 The Chairperson of the MPIADC to ensure that all supplies in
the RPCI and PPEs in the RPCPPE should contain complete
information/identification, like item description, date acquired, serial
numbers, property tags, cost, etc. These data would be helpful in
identifying and comparing the properties between records. These
information can be sourced from the previous years’ records and from
the physical markings of the property. Get the service of an in-house or
independent appraiser, or the appraiser from the province, to provide
cost of the properties with deficient documents;
1.7.3 The Property Custodian to make and maintain a list of all the
supplies and property of the LGU, with complete information thereof
reflected therein. Ensure the ALL property of the LGU shall be
counted and considered in the Report/list. The list shall be reconciled
with the similar records in the accounting at least a quarter;
1.7.4 The Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other
property Ledger Cards, as well as complete list/schedule of the same.
Again, the supplies and property in the list/schedule should bear
complete information as to description and specification. Reconcile
the accounting records with the list/reports being kept by the Property
Custodian or with the MPIADC at least quarterly;
1.7.5 The GSO to property tag every PPE. This will help in easy and
fast monitoring of the assets and identification thereof in case a loss of
the same;
1.7.6 The Property Custodian to provide PAR to every PPE issued to
respective end users. Accountability to the property redounds to the
latter; and
1.7.7 The Management to adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the
NGAS Manual, Volume 1, Section 3(c), Chapter V of the Manual
on Property Custodianship, paragraph 13 of IPSAS No. 17, and other
pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of
comprehensive physical count of supplies and properties, maintenance
of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

2.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust the
Due to LGUs account with the misstated amount of P47,950.12 to present the
same fairly in the financial statements; Exert more effort to dig up available
records and other documents and substantiate the balances which are absent
of the same; and adhere to Sections 4.8, 111.1 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445, as
well as paragraph 7 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards
(IPSAS) No. 1 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 in the recognition, recording
and reporting of the Due from LGUs and Due from NGAs accounts in the
financial statements.

3.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert more
effort to dig up available records and substantiate the balances which are
absent of the same. If creditors are already identified, settle immediately the
payables with the former to avoid financial losses through charges and
penalties, otherwise, close the balances thereof to Equity account; Follow-up
the confirmation made with the creditors who have not yet responded to the
validation. Adjust the account accordingly with the results thereof; Verify
with the creditors those payables with identified negative balances and
collect from them if these prove overpayments, otherwise, close the same to
Equity account; Adjust the Accounts Payable account with the misstated
amount of P284,703.38 to present the same fairly in the financial
statements; and adhere to Sections 4.8, 111.1 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445, as
well as paragraph 7 of International Public Sector Accounting Standards
(IPSAS) No. 1 in the recognition, recording and reporting of the Accounts
Payable account in the financial statements.

4.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
4.6.1 Exert more effort to dig up available records and substantiate
the balances which are absent of the same, as well as those claims which
are deficient of supporting documents. Ask assistance from the
previous LGU accountant for the purpose. Amounts which cannot be
substantiated, despite enormous effort to do so, and which are not
subject of the demand for payment shall be closed to Equity account;
4.6.2 Inquire, through a letter, from the LGU officers and employees,
as well as those from the other creditors like the QUEDANCOR, LBP,
DBP, and BAUG CARP, the existence and validity of the respective
claims. If the liabilities exist and valid, pay them. If not, close the
amount to Equity account;
4.6.3 Recheck available supporting documents for the accounts with
negative balances. Rectify immediately if there were errors noted in the
recording of transactions. Collect from the creditors if there proved to
be overpayments. Again, amounts which cannot be substantiated,
despite enormous effort to do so, shall be closed to Equity account; and
4.6.4 Adhere to Sections 4.8, 111.1 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445, as well as
paragraph 7 of IPSAS No. 1 in the recognition, recording and reporting
of the Due to Officers and Employees, Due to Other NGAs, Due to
LGUs and Other Payables accounts in the financial statements.

5.6 We recommended and the following LGU personnel agreed that:
5.6.1 The Market Committee, through its Chairman, adjudicate all
matters concerning the market based on the provisions of the Market
Code. Submit written suggestion or formulate procedures to observe
with regard to the grant of installments covering the fees being imposed
in the provisions of the Code. These procedures shall include controls
that ensure collection of the receivables. The LEIPO must assist the
Committee on this matter. Submit the same to the Mayor and to the SB
for necessary amendments to the Market Code;
5.6.2 The Market Committee to submit recommendations for
appropriate consideration to the Mayor and to the SB concerning the
obligatory fees that were found onerous by the vendors and for the
latter to study thereof and to make amendments if necessary;
5.6.3 The Market Committee to immediately issue a Certificate of
Award to the market vendors who have found qualified to own a
respective stall in the market;
5.6.4 The Market Supervisor to secure copies of the Application to
Lease the Market Stall and the Contract of Lease that are still on hand
of the market vendors and to make sure that the complete data and
information of all the stall holders are maintained;
5.6.5 The Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books as
receivable the earned goodwill, occupancy privilege fee and block rental
fee that were remained unsettled as of December 31, 2019 in the total
amount of P1,449,200.00;
5.6.6 The Municipal Treasurer to adopt procedures ensuring the
collection of the monthly goodwill, occupancy privilege fee and block
rental fee. The LEIPO may assist the Treasurer in the formulation of
the said procedures;
5.6.7 The Municipal Treasurer to provide a job order to collect
minimal fees from the CR users or the duty to be added to the
responsibilities being performed by the existing personnel assigned in
the market;
5.6.8 The Market Committee to adjudicate every violation committed
by the vendors and to impose corresponding penalty; and
5.6.9 Adhere to pertinent provisions covering the operations of the
public market.

6.5 We recommended that Management stop charging administrative/
operating expenses against the LDRRMF. Submit to COA the legal basis on the
payment of office supplies, travel expenses for DRRM council meetings,
communication and internet expenses in the amount of P52,348.02, the fuels,
oils, lubricants and spare parts for LGU vehicles which were not exclusively
used to support calamities/ disasters activities totaling P589,717.26 as well as
those disbursements for JOs wages and rescuers honorarium amounting to
P645,980.00. Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of the
submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit, this may
result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may
warrant; Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers by
year of transfer as required in Item 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
Advise the MDRRMO to prepare and submit in to COA the Report on Sources
and Utilization of LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No.
2012-002. Assign a personnel to monthly prepare the report so that it will not
be overlooked; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A.
No. 10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Item 4.3 of
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and Item 5.1.12 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 in the budgeting, utilization and reporting of the
LDRRMF.

7.5 We recommended and the MENRO agreed to formulate a regular
garbage collection schedule that covers all the barangays within the
jurisdiction of the Municipality. Suggest to the Barangay or cluster of
barangays, for the latter to designate a specific place that can be easily
accessed by the garbage truck, where they may gather their non-recyclable
and special wastes to ensure collection; Equip the vehicle being used in the
garbage collection with the necessary features required in the collection and
transportation of wastes. If the foregoing not possible, include in the
LDRRMFIP, as basis for the budget preparation, an amount for the
procurement of a vehicle with the prescribed features mentioned in Section 24
of the Act; Allocate funds for the creation of a sanitary landfill through
inclusion thereof in the LDRRMFIP. Follow-up the request of funding made
from the NCCC for the same purpose; Prioritize the establishment of possible
sites for the sanitary landfill so that when funds are already available,
purchase thereof will be made immediately; and faithfully adhere to Sections
10, 24, 37, 40 and 41 of R.A. No. 9003 in the collection, transportation and
disposal of solid waste materials.

8.7 We recommended that the HRMO revise the rates given to the thirty
(30) job orders, from fixed monthly rate to daily or piecework rate basis and
execute a standard form contract with the Job Orders as provided in the CSC
Resolution No. 021480 bearing the necessary information as required therein.

9.5 We recommended that Management call the attention and direct the
concerned officials and employees to immediately submit the respective
Reports, Statements, and other documents to COA in a manner prescribed in
Section 122 of P.D. No. 1445 and Chapter 4 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

10.6 We recommended and the SB agreed that through its presiding officer,
the Vice-Mayor, enact an ordinance promulgating taxes and rental fee rates to
be used in the assessment of utilizing the trading post, taking into
consideration the objectives of the project. Advice the LEIPO Officer, to
draft operating procedures to be adopted in the use of the facility, like the
formulation of market rules, to achieve order and clean trading venue.

11.5 We recommended and the LEIPO Officer agreed to formulate
operating procedures covering the utilization of the municipal gym and
women’s center. These procedures must cover the accomplishment by the
client of a document or application form before the facilities will be used and
which will be the basis also by the treasury for the issuance of OR. The
procedures will also determine who will clean the venue before and after use,
the municipal personnel to maintain the cleanliness and order of the amenities,
grievance measures to follow for any untoward incidents while using the venue
and other procedures to ensure that full economic gain of each of the program
is achieved; The Municipal Agriculturist also agreed to require the rentee to
accomplish the ERRO before the use of the farm tractor; The Municipal
Treasurer likewise agreed to always ask from the clients the application forms
and/or the ERRO as basis for the issuance of OR when payments received.
Applicable rates for the services, as provided in the respective Ordinance shall
always be applied; and the Mayor or the MAO agreed to consider adjusting
the rate of the tractor operator which will commensurate to his labor so as not
to diminish his morale in performing his multiple tasks.

12.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Accountant stop
the practice of procuring and paying goods through reimbursement scheme.
This is impliedly prohibited in Section 10 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Submit
to the Auditor the justification and/or legal basis on the procurements made
through reimbursement in the amount of P373,934.43. Failure to comply, or
after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was
found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the
amount as circumstances may warrant. Ensure adherence to Section 10 of the
IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of various goods of the LGU.

13.7 We recommended that:
13.7.1 The Sangguniang Bayan, with the inputs from the MSWDO,
revise the Municipal Ordinance No. 003 series of 2018 to come up with a set
of detailed and comprehensive guidelines on the budgeting, utilization and
reporting of the fund allocated for the program. The Ordinance must include
the legal basis of the program, the objectives thereof, the complete
requirements as to who and how to avail the skolaran which includes
residence of the recipient, social classification, annual income, course to
take if in college, etc. This likewise states the information on the
responsibilities of the LGU in granting the educational assistance,
responsibilities of the recipient for the amount received which include the
grade to maintain, documents to present as proof of exemplary performance,
etc., and the procedures to take if any party in the agreement is at fault to
any of the provisions;
13.7.2 The Municipal Mayor, with the authority from the Sangguniang
Bayan and assistance from the MSWDO, execute a MOA and enter into an
agreement with each of the scholars so that the funds are safeguarded
against misuse and to remind each party of the duties and responsibilities
attached to the grant;
13.7.3 The MSWDO to furnish COA the list of LGU scholars detailing
their basic biological information and other facts in compliance to the
requirements set forth in Section 447(5)(xi) of R.A. No. 7160 and MO No.
003 series of 2018;
13.7.4 The Municipal Mayor, Accountant and MSWDO to submit to
the Auditor justification on the payments of tuition and other fees to
Mr. Bustillo and not the monthly P700.00 allowance as stated in the
Ordinance. Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of
the submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit,
this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as
circumstances may warrant; and
13.7.5 The Management faithfully adhere to the intent of Section
447(5)(xi) of R.A No. 7160, Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and with the
Municipal Ordinance No. 003 series of 2018 in the implementation of the
Skolaran Program.

14.6 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed that, with the
assistance from the Accountant, Treasurer, MBO and the MSWDO, execute
comprehensive guidelines/policies and individual MOA governing the financial
assistance paid to various schools. These guidelines/policies and MOAs must
detail, among others, the legal basis of the program, the purpose or objective
thereof, how the funds will be availed including its source, responsibilities of
the LGU in disbursing the funds, responsibilities of the schools as beneficiary
of the assistance, and measures to be taken for any violation to the provisions;
The Accountant also agreed to ensure that relevant information regarding the
transactions must be provided in the voucher and/or attached to the payment;
The MBO likewise agreed to ascertain that the appropriation made for the
program must be specific and determinable therein; and faithfully adhere to
the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No.
2012-001 in the grant of financial assistance to various schools in the LGU.

15.2 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to determine
the details of the beginning balances of the unremitted taxes and make the
necessary adjustments to reconcile the balances.

16.2 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the
concerned persons liable to settle their disallowances and the concerned
responsible persons to comply with the requirements of their suspensions in
accordance with the Revised RRSA.

We have recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Treasury to complete its physical inventory and to submit immediately the inventory report in compliance with Section 124 Volume I of NGAS Manual. Also, please require the Municipal Treasury to maintain its property cards as required under the above provision of the NGAS Manual.

We have further recommended for the Accounting Office to prepare its property ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation of physical inventory balances with that of the Municipal Treasury Office records.

We have recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to comply with the requirements of Section 68 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 in imposing liquidated damages for breach of contract.

We have further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) explain in writing on the advance deliveries made on the purchase of office supplies and office equipment.

Strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations governing the preparation of the ABC and the procurement of infrastructure projects pursuant to DPWH DO No. 197 and DOLE DO No. 13 series of 1998.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to, as much as possible, realistically increase the estimated collections for the garbage fees and penalties based on the trend of actual collections made in the past years. Instruct the MENRO to optimally utilize the LGU dump truck to collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes from all its barangays. observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We have recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Treasury to complete its physical inventory and to submit immediately the inventory report in compliance with Section 124 Volume I of NGAS Manual. Also, please require the Municipal Treasury to maintain its property cards as required under the above provision of the NGAS Manual.
We have further recommended for the Accounting Office to prepare its property ledger cards to facilitate the reconciliation of physical inventory balances with that of the Municipal Treasury Office records.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly abide by the provisions set forth in the Section 54.2 of RA 9184 on PhilGEPS posting.  We further recommend for the BAC to submit written explanation on the inconsistencies noted in the above disbursements.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management direct the concerned officials to comply with the requirements of Section 68 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 in imposing liquidated damages for breach of contract.
We have also recommended the submission of the Consular Invoice/Pro-forma Invoice of the foreign supplier and Breakdown of the expenses incurred in the Importation for further evaluation.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the BAC judiciously observe proper procurement procedures pursuant to the provisions set forth in the revised IRR of RA 9184.
We have further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) explain in writing on the advance deliveries made on the purchase of office supplies and office equipment.

Recommendation:
In view of the above cited deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
Submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted for the evaluation of the Audit Team as basis for the next audit action.
Strictly adhere to the laws, rules and regulations governing the preparation of the ABC and the procurement of infrastructure projects pursuant to DPWH DO No. 197 and DOLE DO No. 13 series of 1998.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the official or employee designated to monitor projects submit written justification on the delayed completion of the above-named project. Likewise, projects must be monitored diligently to avoid incurrence of delays on subsequent projects.
We have further recommended for the Municipal Engineer to compute the liquidated damages and impose the same as well as other penalty provisions contained in the contract for breach of terms and conditions on the above project

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure proper planning so that all its programmed development projects during the year will be implemented for the benefit of the constituents.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the GAD Focal Point System duly created prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) for proper monitoring and evaluation of accomplishments as well as identifying the major constraints or problems encountered.

1.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly as prescribed in Section 181(c) of the GAAM, Volume I.  Reconciliation of their books includes effecting therein the reconciling items reflected in the BRS and from other possible reports/records of financial transactions; instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit to COA for verification the BRS for the LGU’s depository accounts together with the paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memoranda in a manner prescribed in Item 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and adhere to Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 181(c) of the GAAM, and Item 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 to present reliable cash balances in the financial statements.

2.7	We recommended that Management constitute an Inventory Committee through an issuance of an executive order by the LCE; direct the Committee to spearhead the planning and to conduct physical inventory of all the LGUs supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to COA the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the Report on Physical Count of PPE and other properties pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; create a plantilla or designate a GSO who will be in-charged in monitoring the received and disposition of the procured supplies and properties of the LGU.  Recommend with the GSO/Supply Officer the adoption of storing supplies equivalent to a 3-month consumption.  This way, the procured supplies will be delivered to the GSO and not directly to the end users and the utilization of stock cards will be practiced.  The GSO/Supply Officer shall also make and keep list or record of all the supplies and properties of the LGU which will be updated from time to time.  The list will be reconciled with the records from the accounting office; advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards.  Reconcile the accounting records with the list/reports being kept by the GSO/Supply Officer at least quarterly; direct the GSO/Supply Officer to practice property tagging and issue ARE for each of the property given to the end users; and adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

3.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to stop charging administrative/ operating expenses against the LDRRMF.  Submit to COA the legal basis on the payment of office supplies, cellcards, spare parts and oils and lubricants for LGU vehicles which were not exclusively used for calamities/disasters in the total amount of P279,842.67, as well as those disbursements for JOs wages and rescuers honorarium totaling P406,654.00.  Non-compliance hereof, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; direct the MDRRMO to exclude in the LDRRMFIP the illegitimate PAPs as mentioned in Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; advise the MDRRMO to optimally use the LDRRMF.  Include only in the LDRRMFIP very important/urgent PAPs which needed to be executed during the year, with due consideration of the time and manpower constraints.  The MDRRMO must also be reminded that the previous years’ budget for the disaster fund had corresponding PAPs to implement.  Thus, the appropriation thereof should be utilized as intended and never to be used to fulfill other PAPs; instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized/unexpended LDRRMF at year-end to the special trust fund.  The unused amount can be determined from the cashbook since the amount appropriated for the LDRRM as well as the charges thereof are duly recorded therein.  The amount can also be verified from the records being maintained by the MDRRMO; direct the Municipal Accountant to accrue and transfer the unutilized/unexpended LDRRMF totaling P1,242,737.83 to the special trust fund and to record such transfer in the respective books.  The procedure was duly prescribed in Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.  The same COA Circular mandates the Accountant in Item 5.1.12 thereof to maintain subsidiary ledgers by year of transfer; and ensure compliance to the provisions of Section 2 of R.A. No. 10121, Item 4 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the budgeting, utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

4.5	We recommended that Management direct the MENRO-Designate to implement the annual programs and activities indicated in the ESWMP; tell the MENRO and his office to encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays, through the conduct of seminars, symposia or dialogues, to create and implement their ESWMP and to submit to the MENRO the needed reports, as indicated in the Plan, for consolidation and monitoring purposes.  This would also address the problem on proper segregation of solid wastes by the barangays and the responsibility of the municipality to only collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes therefrom; advise the MSWMB, headed by the Municipal Mayor, to amend, if possible, the provisions of the ESWMP, to include therein the information as to what Fund the collections of fees and charges will accrue and to what specific purpose these will be used.  If not possible, propose to the Sangguniang Bayan the amendments to the Solid Waste Management Code of the Municipality to include therein the same information.  This would establish as to what and where to source the amount needed to implement the programs and activities in the Plan; direct the Municipal Treasurer to, as much as possible, realistically increase the estimated collections for the garbage fees and penalties based on the trend of actual collections made in the past years.  This would be the basis and guidance in budgeting an amount needed to realize the programs in the Plan; instruct the MENRO to optimally utilize the LGU dumptruck to collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes from all its barangays.  This responsibility is clearly mandated in Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003.  Those barangays that are near-to-impossible to be visited/reached by the garbage truck for garbage collection should be informed thoroughly and meticulously on how to dispose their non-recyclable materials and special wastes; and remind the MENRO to strictly adhere to the provision of Section 10 of R.A. No. 9003.  This likewise warrants the MENRO to advise the respective barangay officials of all its barangays to comply faithfully the provision.  This way, the collection of solid wastes which were left unsegregated in an open dump site will be avoided.  Strict compliance to audit recommendation number 2 would also be a remedy to the problem.

5.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to advise the GFPS to complete and update the information provided in the LGU’s GAD database; direct the GFPS to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of JMC No. 2013-01; stop utilizing the GAD Funds for transactions that are inconsistent with the GAD activities and which unparalleled the corresponding performance indicators.  These activities and performance indicators were formulated to realize each GAD PAPs; (4) Submit to the COA Audit Team a copy of the GPB in the manner prescribed in Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and strictly adhere to the governing provisions set forth in the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 in the creation, utilization and reporting of GAD fund.

6.5	We recommended that Management stop granting salary differential to the JOs that has no specific statutory provision authorizing thereof, nor anchored on the legitimate determination of the amount wherein a new wage rate is compared with an old rate, and violates the basic principles of contracting; submit to COA the legal basis/justification on the payment made totaling P541,862.00.  Failure to comply, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445, Article 1305, Chapter 1, Title II of the Civil Code, and Section 6.3.1, of CSC-COA-DBM JC No. 1 s. 2017 in the payment of salaries/compensation to JOs.

7.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to prepare the PMR in the form prescribed by the GPPB.  The accomplished form shall be forwarded to the HoPE for her review and approval.  The HoPE, after her approval, shall submit the Report to the GPPB in a manner prescribed in Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

8.5	We recommended that Management stop the practice of procuring goods through reimbursement scheme.  This is impliedly prohibited in Section 10 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  Submit to the Audit Team the justification and/or legal basis on the procurements made through reimbursement in the amount of P99,421.09   Failure to comply, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; and ensure adherence to Section 10 of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of various goods of the LGU.

9.5	We recommended that Management direct the person concerned to submit to COA the Activity Design covering each meals and/or snacks paid;  ensure that all official receipt received in exchange of the amount paid for official transactions should bear the name of the LGU; refrain from procurement of meals and/or snacks through reimbursement scheme.  Submit to COA the legal basis/justification on the payments made through reimbursement totaling P133,180.32.  Failure to comply, or after evaluation by this end of the submitted justification and the same was found wanting of merit, this may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount as circumstances may warrant; and adhere to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 10 of R.A. No. 9184 in the payments of meals and/or snacks.

10.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the accounting and treasury office to submit to the Auditor the lacking/missing DVS without delay.  Failure to comply may warrant the automatic withholding of the salaries of the personnel accountable thereof pursuant to Section 122 (2) of P.D. No. 1445 and/or a ground for the filing of an administrative disciplinary action under Section 127 of the Decree.  Strictly adhere to Section 100 of P.D. No. 1445, Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 44 of NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I, in the reporting and submission of the accounts to the Auditor.

11.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to adhere to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of perfected contracts together with the related supporting documents to COA for review.  The BAC or the BAC Secretariat must submit to COA the bidding documents together with the perfected contracts in a manner prescribed in the foregoing-mentioned COA Circular.  It is not the responsibility of the accounting nor the engineering office to perform the submission to the Audit Team.

12.7	We recommended that Management initiate an intensive dialogue with the contractor and find doable and effective solutions to the problem if still possible; rescind the contract with Charlz Construction if curative actions to the problem is beyond realizable.  Seek legal assistance from the lawyer of the province or the component city if they have one for the judicial rescission process; forfeit the performance security.  Send a demand letter addressed to the Bank Guarantor to pay the LGU the amount of P2,062,704.65 as agreed upon in the Performance Security (Bank Guarantee).  It is already provided in the Bank Guarantee that the Guarantor will pay the Agency the amount agreed upon during the first demand without cavil or argument or without the municipality needing to prove or to show grounds or reasons for the demand.  Seek legal assistance from the lawyer of the city or province if necessary; and  ensure the adherence to Article 1191 of Civil Code and Section 39.3 of R.A. No. 9184 in relation to Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 if the matter at hand involves abandonment by the contractors, without valid causes, in the projects implementation.
13.2	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to determine the details of the beginning balances of the unremitted taxes and make the necessary adjustments to reconcile the balances.

14.2	We recommended and the Management agreed to require the concerned persons liable to settle their disallowances and the concerned responsible persons to comply with the requirements of their suspensions in accordance with the Revised RRSA.

15.6	We recommended that Management evaluate whether there could still be funds available to be assisted to Barangay Mayag for the procurement of culverts so that the deficiencies noted on the project could be rectified as soon as possible;  remind the Municipal Engineer to always include in the POW all the materials needed to complete the project so that it could be used effectively;  enjoin the MPDO to check the projects/programs whether the supporting POW or activity design are supportive of the corresponding purpose or objective.  As an office of check and balance, the MPDO has one of its duties of making sure that all of the LGU programs, projects and activities are working correctly and effectively; and adhere to Item 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 and Section 2 of P.D. 1445 in the implementation of various projects, programs and activities of the municipality under the 20% development fund.

16.7	We recommended that Management revisit the multi-purpose building and re-evaluate its condition vis-à-vis the program of work.  Communicate with the contractor the deficiency noted, if there is any, so that immediate corrective measure shall be done; turn over the building, as soon as possible, to the barangay so that this could be used the soonest as intended; and adhere to Item 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 and Section 2 of P.D. 1445 in the implementation of various projects, programs and activities of the municipality under the 20% development fund.

17.6	We recommended that Management identify what are the first and basic requirements/things needed for the project to operate and sustain.   These basic necessities should be accomplished first before realizing the next parts, items or level of the projects.  Adherence to this method avoids unnecessary expenses that may incur in the maintenance, re-cleaning or adjustments to the project due to the delay in its operation.  Likewise, observance to the system evades sustaining wear and tear of the project without getting economic benefits therefrom; fast tract the bidding process conducted for the procurement of the water system; commence without delay the bidding process for the purchase of the electrical facility.  Unnecessary deferral on its acquisition may incur additional and excessive costs to the LGU; and adhere to Item 5 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017 and Section 2 of P.D. 1445 in the implementation of various projects, programs and activities of the municipality under the 20% development fund.

18.7	We recommended and the Management agreed to request the Contractor to finish the project in accordance with the POW without incurring further delay especially the provision of water facility.   The lavatories and hygiene facilities must be cleaned from cement smudges too so that these will be ready for use.  Further, the water pool in the hygiene facility must be remedied also, just like putting a roof over it, to avoid accident that may cause to the users; remind the Municipal Engineer to ensure that before accepting completed projects from contractors, the same should already finished in accordance with the POW and ready to be used by the intended beneficiaries; enjoin the MPDO to check from time to time the projects/programs of the LGU whether these were implemented in accordance with the POW and are ready for use by the recipients once completed and accepted by the ME from the contractor; and adhere to Item 1 of DILG MC No. 2018-61 and other future issuances covering the implementation of LGSF-AM program, as well as Section 2 of P.D. 1445 to ensure that government programs, projects and activities were implemented effectively and as planned.

I. We have recommended that Property Cards should be kept and maintained by the Local Treasurer to record the acquisition, description, custody, disposal and other information such as the physical condition and existence for each class of PPE in pursuant to Section 119 of the MNGAS to be used in the reconciliation with the results of the physical inventory count.

For the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of physical inventory count and complete the preparation of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to be submitted to the Auditor in compliance with Section 124 of the MNGAS and in consonance with Section 156 of COA Circular 92-386.
observations.reason: Conduct of Inventory was already done on PPE pertaining to SEF. However, conduct of PPEs in the General Fund is yet to be started.

observations.recommendation: II. We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the details of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary adjusting entry/ies to reconcile to its reciprocal account in order to conform with sound internal control policies and Generally Accepted Accounting Principles.

III. We have recommended for the municipal accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the amount of P900,000.00 to the appropriate asset account and to closely monitor projects still in progress to avoid incurrence of delay in the reporting of completed projects.

IV. We have recommended that Management refrain from the practice of paying and charging prior year expenses to current year appropriation for proper matching of income and expenditures to be able to reflect the correct balance of net income for a particular period. This is in adherence with the above-quoted provisions of R.A. No.7160 and PD 1445.
Expenses of the previous year paid on the current year should be recorded in the books with the following entry:
On the year expense was incurred
Dr. 	Expense Account		xxx
	Cr.	Accounts Payable		xxx 
On the year expense was paid
Dr. 	Accounts Payable		xxx
		Cr.	Cash		xxx 
Likewise, we have recommended that Management ensure that the obligations on utility bills are settled on or before due dates to avoid penalties or surcharges. Relatively, the responsible official/employee should refund the surcharge of P1,715.26.

V. We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should to the fullest extent possible, ensure that all programs/ projects listed in its Annual Investment Program be implemented to give the municipal constituents the maximum benefits that could be derived and to adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We have recommended that Management refrain from the practice of paying and charging prior year expenses to current year appropriation for proper matching of income and expenditures to be able to reflect the correct balance of net income for a particular period. This is in adherence with the above-quoted provisions of R.A. No.7160 and PD 1445.
Expenses of the previous year paid on the current year should be recorded in the books with the following entry:
On the year expense was incurred
Dr.	Expense Account		xxx
Cr.	Accounts Payable		xxx
On the year expense was paid
Dr.	Accounts Payable		xxx
Cr.	Cash			  xxx
Likewise, we have recommended that Management ensure that the obligations on utility bills are settled on or before due dates to avoid penalties or surcharges. Relatively, the responsible official/employee should refund the surcharge of P1,715.26.

We have recommended that the municipal officials concerned should to the fullest extent possible, ensure that all programs/ projects listed in its Annual Investment Program be implemented to give the municipal constituents the maximum benefits that could be derived and to adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We have recommended that management implement and enforce the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program by utilizing the funds appropriated for the said purpose to fully attain the objectives set forth under Republic Act 9003.

We have recommended that management avail of the remedies provided in the Local Government Code with which to collect the tax delinquencies in order to generate more income for the LGU. Further, such action to collect should be within the time prescribed. Otherwise, the LGU shall be estopped from collecting because the action to collect has expired.

We have recommended that the concerned officials make proper evaluation relative to the use of public market to ensure that maximum benefits could be derived from the said property.
For prospective projects, management should conduct an in-depth study on projects to be undertaken to ensure that said projects will deliver utmost advantage to the constituents and are optimized for the purpose for which they will be constructed.

We have recommended that GAD Plan and Budget as well as the Annual Accomplishment Report must be submitted to the PPDO for review and monitoring purposes.  Likewise, full implementation of the GAD Plan and Budget is also recommended in order to realize the intended result and extending the expected benefits to beneficiaries.

For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended the following:

Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed asset and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.

Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, PARs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.

For control and monitoring make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
For the proper management and handling of the PPE, we have recommended the following:
Conduct the required annual physical count of the PPE as of December of every year. The actual count may be undertaken any month during the current year, but has to be updated at year-end. This procedure is a must in order to know the presence/physical existence of the fixed asset and their conditions; and to identify, segregate and reclassify unserviceable assets. Reconciliation of the result of the count with the accounting records is to be undertaken to effect the necessary adjustments and to pin point responsibility/accountability/liability for missing PPEs.
Maintain/update the subsidiary ledgers, PPE ledger cards, PARs and placing of property tags which are required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPEs and for facility in identifying them.
For control and monitoring make use of ICS for semi-expendable items to avoid loss, as these are usually small items.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, to consistently monitor the granting, utilization as well as check the propriety, validity and completeness of the supporting documents needed in the liquidation of these cash advances.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording and issuance of supplies, record the same thru the Inventory account pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS and  PPSAS 12 on Inventories to uphold fair presentation of the Inventory account in the FS.
The following are the suggested pro-forma entries to take up inventory transactions:
Dr.	Supplies/Materials Inventory	xxx
Cr.	Cash In Bank			xxx
Due to BIR				xxx
To record acquisition and payment of supplies/materials
Dr.	  Supplies/Materials Expense	xxx
Cr.	Supplies/Materials Inventory	xxx
To record the issuance of supplies and users

Recommendation:
We have recommended that utilization of GAD Fund be maximizedand that proper planning be made so that all GAD related projects, programs and activities during the year be implemented immediately for the full attainment of its benefits beneficial to their constituents.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund and ensure proper planning so that all its programmed development projects during the year will be implemented for the benefit of its constituents.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management avoid reimbursement as a mode of procurement and adopt public bidding or other alternative modes of procurement provided under RA 9184, in order to promote transparency and to avoid possible source of irregularities.
We also have recommended that regular purchase of various materials, supplies and equipments by the Municipality should be made through check disbursement direct to the supplier/creditor.
Likewise, compliance with BIR regulations should be adhered to by the Municipal Accounting Office on withholding of taxes.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Management require the concerned officials to exert utmost efforts to meet the demands of timely reporting and submission of accounts. This is to avoid undue delay precluding the team from timely conduct of audit and rendering of the required reports in compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations. Reports when submitted on time are useful information that can aid management in decision making.

Recommendation:
We have recommended for the management to exert more effort to collect these real property tax delinquencies especially those that have been long overdue for the municipality to have an additional income that could be used to fund various projects and programs that would be beneficial to its constituents.

1.5	We recommended that Agency direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I, to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

2.6	We recommended that Management require the Accountant to make a journal entry to restore the cash for the equivalent amount of the stale checks to reflect the correct cash balance as of December 31, 2016; Advice the Municipal Treasurer to inform or inquire with the payees on the reasons why the checks remained   unpresented to the drawee bank.  The action will preclude the payees from claims of damages and for the municipality to have an immediate recourse just in case some checks were issued to unauthorized persons; and Adhere to Section 59 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in the proper treatment of stale checks in the records of the Agency.

3.7	We recommended that Management create a plantilla or designate a GSO in accordance with the provisions of Article 20 of R.A. 7160; Constitute an inventory committee/team.  The team will spearhead, plan and conduct physical count of the Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on Physical Count of PPE and other properties pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs and other properties Ledger Cards;  Advise the Storekeeper to maintain Supplies and Property Cards per category of materials, PPEs and other properties to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records regularly; Adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

4.6	We recommended that Management construct or maintain a secured shelter or room where to store the unserviceable properties awaiting for disposal; Create a disposal committee who will spearhead in planning and implementing disposal procedures in accordance with the existing guidelines of disposing government properties; Dispose all unserviceable properties per recommendation of the disposal committee for added income of the LGU; Inspect and evaluate from time to time whether these private vehicles in the municipal garage are occupying for an official business.  Private vehicles without legit purpose should be returned to the prospective owner.  The space vacated shall be utilized for municipal vehicles only.  Municipal vehicles should be always parked in the covered garage when not in use; and Strictly observe Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in utilizing government scarce resources.

5.5	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to identify the reconciling items of the affected reciprocal accounts and effect the adjustments thereof in the respective books to present fairly the said accounts in the financial statements.

6.5 	We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to base the estimated RPT Receivable/Deferred RPT and SET Receivable/ Deferred SET from the RPT Account Register which contains the names of the real property taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjustments/reconciling items in the respective books to present fairly the balances of the accounts in the financial statements; and Adhere to the provisions of Section 112 of P.D. No. 1445, Sections 233 and 235 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I in setting up, collections, recording and presentation of the RPT Receivable/Deferred RPT and SET Receivable/Deferred SET accounts in the financial statements.

7.6	We recommended that Management prepare and issue a well-defined set of guidelines/policy governing the implementation of AICS program which must be aligned with the provisions of DSWD Administrative Orders No. 5 and 1 s. 2008 and 2011, respectively; Devolve to the MSWD the assessment of the beneficiaries and determination of the grants to be given under the program.  Payments thereof may be assigned to the Treasury; Prepare a determinable appropriation specifically for the AICS program; Instruct the municipal treasurer, through the recommendation of the MSWDO, that grants involving more than P5,000.00 must be given checks instead of cash; Supporting documents to the grants must be complete and duly filled up; Advise the MSWD that keeping of records on AICS program is paramount as one of the standard operating procedures in government transactions; and Faithfully adhere to the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of any program of the LGU.

8.7	We recommended that Sangguniang Bayan (SB) prepare and issue a set of well-defined guidelines/policies to govern the implementation of the Eskolaran Program of the LGU. The guidelines must equivocally provide procedures on who will qualify for the program, what are the requirements needed to qualify, what grade to maintain to remain in the program; documents needed to claim the financial assistance, remedies to avail incase a scholar has faulted any of the guidelines, among others; Management  prepare a determinable appropriation specifically for the Eskolaran program; and  Management faithfully adhere to the intent of Section 447(5)(xi) of R.A No. 7160 and Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of any program of the LGU.

9.5	We recommended that Management execute comprehensible guidelines/policies or MOA governing voluntary contributions by the LGU to various schools; Ensure that payments of voluntary contributions to various schools must be debited to Due from NGAs to maintain accountability; and Faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 and Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial aids to various individuals and/or institutions.

10.6	We recommended that Management immediately call the attention of the department heads concerned to timely prepare and post to the bulletin boards allotted for the purpose the required documents/reports enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 in a manner prescribed in the provisions thereof.

11.5	We recommended that Management: (1) Create a plantilla for GSO or designate an employee to the position; (2) Encourage the GSO to practice stocking of a three (3)-month supplies so that immediate needs thereof will be addressed; (3) Advise the BAC to prepare the Abstract of Canvass and forward the same to the GSO for the preparation of the PO; (4) Instruct the GSO to prepare the PO with proper and complete information and have it conformed with the supplier.  Remind the GSO to tell the supplier that delivery of the supplies procured must be made direct to the GSO and in accordance with the terms and conditions stated in the PO; (5) Require the Inspectorate Team to inspect the items delivered and prepare IAR if found in order.  Otherwise, return the goods to the supplier if found defective.  The IAR must be given to the GSO for its signature and furnish it, together with the PO and delivery receipt to the Accounting for recording.  It may be allowed that the end-user will prepare the ObR and DV but its preparation must be made after the delivery of the goods and the information therein must be based from the PO, IAR and delivery receipt; (6) Inform the GSO that the received supplies must be properly kept, segregated and tagged and issuance thereof to the end-user must be recorded in a manner provided in Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU; and (7) Faithfully adhere to Section 122 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I in resolving other matters relating to procurement of supplies and materials.

12.5	We recommended that Management: (1) Encourage every barangay or cluster of barangays to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation, composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste materials will take place. This will aid the Municipality to perform their responsibility which is to collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes in compliance with Section 10 of the Act; (2) Provide assistance to every barangay on information regarding the requirements for the segregation and storage of solid waste as stated in Section 22 of the Republic Act; and (3) Ensure compliance to the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specially Sections 10, 22, 32 and 33 thereof.

13.5	We recommended that Management: (1) Stop the practice of transferring appropriation from one item (account) to another item except if the conditions mentioned in Section 336 of R.A 7160 allow and the requirements stated therein are followed; (2) Refrain from paying expenditures without appropriation.  Only expenses duly budgeted shall be obligated and paid; (3) Submit to the Auditor the legal basis on the augmentation made without determined savings and SB Ordinance.  Failure to comply may cause this Office to issue Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as circumstances may warrant; and (4) Adhere to Section 336 of R.A. 7160 and Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 in the utilization, augmentation or realignment of appropriation.

14.5 	We recommended that Management: (1) Submit to the Auditor the required Certification from the head of the agency that the actual travel expenses were absolutely necessary in the performance of an assignment.  Failure to comply may cause the issuance of a Notice of Suspension or Disallowance on the transactions; (2) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments of actual travel expenses must be supported with the aforementioned Certification, among others; and  (3) Adhere strictly to Section 4 of E.O. No. 298.

15.6	We recommended that Management: (1) Provide at least 5% of the total annual budget for GAD PAPs as prescribed by the JMC; (2) Formulate and include only GAD PAPs that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; (3) Carefully plan, identify and consider gender issues and concerns in the LGU that these may be included in the GPB, with due consideration that optimal utilization of the appropriated amount for the program will be achieved;  (4) The proposed GAD activity, which can either be client- or organization-focused, should be stated as clearly as possible to facilitate budgeting; (5) Submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB in prescribed form and in a manner stated in Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC and Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001; and (5) Strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.
16.2	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should determine the details of the beginning balances of the unremitted taxes and pay the same to the BIR without delay.

17.2	We recommended that Management require the concerned persons liable to settle their disallowances and the concerned responsible persons to comply with the requirements of their suspensions in accordance with the Revised RRSA.

18.7	We recommended that Management identify the cause/s of the delay and find possible solutions to speed up the implementation of the projects and improve performance rates; and draw up implementation timeliness to serve as guide in monitoring the status of project implementation, administration and program management and enhance monitoring activities in order to effect prompt attainment of the programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management:                           a. Otherwise, the Commission on Audit will have to submit the reports of unliquidated cash advances, in aggregate amounts of P50,000.00 and above per accountable officer, to the Civil Service Commission, Philippine Anti-Graft Commission, Office of the Ombudsman or Department of Justice for the filing of appropriate charges.                                         c. Require the Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting journal entries to reclassify the advances for intelligence fund amounting to P630,000.00 by debiting Advances to Special Disbursing Officer and crediting Advances to Officers and Employees with the same amount.
observations.managementAction: This AOM is noted. The 630,000 advances for intelligence fund was re classified to proper account. The advances for travel is now minimized.
