Unliquidated cash advances of Engr. Abarca remained above 50,000.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to require the Municipal Accountant:                                                                    

a. To follow strictly the provisions on the keeping of accounts particularly in the recognition of liability and payment of liabilities to ensure that adequate, accurate or not misleading information are furnished to users of the financial statements and the public as well pursuant to Section 111 of P.D. No. 1445;                                    

b. To submit the subsidiary ledgers and list of unpaid claimants for 20% Development Fund, Calamity Fund, Waterworks System, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund-Proper in the total amount of P496,881.18;              

c. To include only bona fide and legitimate claims of suppliers/creditors in the Accounts Payable account. Likewise, let him explain why current expenditures of P116,110.00 were charged to Accounts Payable as of CY 2014 contrary to Section 4 (1) of P.D. No. 1445, Section 328 of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 46 (2) of P.D. No. 1177.                                                              

d. To prepare adjusting journal entries for the unrecorded payments in CY 2015 on disbursement vouchers of CY 2014 totalling P48,710.74 and for the over deductions of Accounts Payable account and double recording of Due to BIR accounts amounting to P8,411.69 upon payment to the suppliers/creditors. to prepare separate JEV on the payments of Accounts Payable and do not detach the JEV in the recognition of liability account.
                                                                                  e. To submit all unpaid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents that were booked up as Accounts Payables as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction: The expenditures noted by the auditors were transactions of 2014 paid in 2015 which are part of the payables in 2014. However, it is recorded at gross since the actual date of payment to suppliers  cannot be determined or some vouchers were not actually claimed. Observations of the auditor were noted and adjustment will be taken if necessary. No accounts payable were booked up in 2015. The paid claims of 48,710.74 were actually charged in the budget of 2015 and recorded as regular expense in 2015. Adjustment will be effected as recommended by the auditors.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented

Subsidiary ledger for accounts payable was submitted but no schedule of unpaid claimants and unpaid disbursement vouchers was submitted to this Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers concerned to prioritize the liquidation of fund transfers for every project. Only part of the whole amount because the trust liabilities is the only account in trust where in the excess of income over expenses in trust can be equited.

Not Implemented

No liquidations recorded in the books of the LGU per review of the ledgers.

 c. Direct the Municipal Accountant not to obligate amount for any claims which is beyond its specific purpose and not to process claims not supported with complete documents.

observations.recommendation:

1) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and forwarded the same to the Accounting Office together with the disbursement vouchers and other supporting documents.

Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39 (1&3) of P.D. No. 1445.

Partially Implemented

Not all cash advances were liquidated.

observations.reason:

Clerk wages remained charged against the SEF for CY 2016.

Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. II. Not Implemented

Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to check and review the completeness of the supporting documents and of the required information therein.

Fully Implemented

The practice of splitting contracts has been stopped.
